label: Vercel
description: Arcade.dev tools for interacting with Vercel

tools.name: AddProjectDomain
tools.description: Attach a custom domain to a project; the result reports its verification status.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name to attach the domain to.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Fully-qualified domain name to attach (e.g. app.example.com).

parameters.name: redirect
parameters.description: Domain to redirect this domain to. Defaults to None (serve the project directly).

parameters.name: git_branch
parameters.description: Git branch to bind the domain to. Defaults to None (the production branch).

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: CancelDeployment
tools.description:
Cancel an in-progress deployment.

Canceling a deployment that has already finished is not an error: its current (terminal) state
is returned unchanged rather than reporting a cancellation.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Deployment id (dpl_...) to cancel.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: CreateDeployment
tools.description:
Trigger a new deployment from inline source files, a git repository, or a prior deployment.

The target project must already exist (create it first); an unknown project name or id is
rejected rather than silently auto-created. Exactly one source is required: pass files to
upload source inline (no Git connection needed, the way to ship a first deployment on an
account without a connected repository), git_repo together with git_ref to deploy from a
connected repository, or redeploy_of to rebuild a prior deployment's source. Deploying from a
repository requires the Vercel account to have a connected Git integration for that provider.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Project name the deployment belongs to.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name to deploy. Defaults to None (inferred from name).

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Environment to deploy to. Selecting preview, or leaving this as the default None, creates a preview deployment, with one exception: a project's very first deployment is always promoted to production by Vercel regardless of this value, so an omitted target on a project that has no prior deployment still goes live in production.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: Source files to deploy inline, without any connected Git repository. Each entry is a file path and its UTF-8 text content. Use this to ship a first deployment for a project that has no Git integration. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: git_repo
parameters.description: Repository to deploy from, as owner/repo. Defaults to None (use files or redeploy_of instead).

parameters.name: git_ref
parameters.description: Branch name or commit SHA to deploy from the repository. Required whenever git_repo is set (Vercel does not infer a default branch). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: git_provider
parameters.description: Git host the repository lives on. Defaults to github.

parameters.name: redeploy_of
parameters.description: Deployment id to redeploy. Rebuilds that deployment's source while picking up the project's current environment variables and build settings, so use this to roll out config changes (env vars edited after a deployment do not affect the running build until a rebuild). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: CreateProject
tools.description: Create a new project, optionally connecting a git repository to deploy from.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the new project (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens).

parameters.name: framework
parameters.description: Framework preset slug (e.g. nextjs, vite, sveltekit). Defaults to None (auto-detect).

parameters.name: git_repository
parameters.description: Repository to connect, as owner/repo (e.g. acme/marketing-site). Connecting a repository is what lets you deploy and scope preview variables to a branch. Defaults to None (no repository connected).

parameters.name: git_provider
parameters.description: Git host the connected repository lives on. Defaults to github.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: DeleteEnvironmentVariable
tools.description: Delete an environment variable from a project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name the variable belongs to.

parameters.name: env_id
parameters.description: Id of the environment variable to delete.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: DeleteProject
tools.description: Permanently delete a project and all of its deployments.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or project name to delete.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: GetCurrentUser
tools.description:
Return the Vercel account the access token authenticates as.

Call this first in a session to confirm which account you are acting on
before making any changes.

tools.name: GetDeployment
tools.description: Return a single deployment's state and metadata.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Deployment id (dpl_...) or deployment URL to inspect.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: GetDeploymentLogs
tools.description: Read a deployment's build and runtime log events to diagnose why it failed.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Deployment id (dpl_...) or deployment URL to read logs for.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log events to return (1-1000). Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Order to read events in. Defaults to forward (oldest first).

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only return events created at or after this epoch-millisecond time. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Only return events created at or before this epoch-millisecond time. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: GetProject
tools.description: Return a single project's settings and latest deployment state.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or project name to inspect.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: GetProjectDomain
tools.description: Return a project domain's verification status and DNS-configuration state.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name the domain is attached to.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Fully-qualified domain name to inspect.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: GetTeam
tools.description: Return a team's details, including its billing plan.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to look up.

tools.name: ListDeployments
tools.description: List deployments in the active scope, optionally filtered by project, target, and state.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name to scope to. Defaults to None (all projects in scope).

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Filter to deployments for this environment. Defaults to None (any environment).

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter to deployments in this state. Defaults to None (any state).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of deployments to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior call's next_cursor. Defaults to None (first page).

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: ListEnvironmentVariables
tools.description: List a project's environment variables across all environments.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name to list variables for.

parameters.name: decrypt
parameters.description: Whether to include decrypted plaintext values in the result (only effective when the plan supports decryption). Defaults to False.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: ListProjectDomains
tools.description: List the custom domains attached to a project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name to list domains for.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of domains to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior call's next_cursor. Defaults to None (first page).

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: ListProjects
tools.description: List the projects in the active scope, optionally narrowed by a fuzzy name search.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive name fragment to filter projects by. Only projects whose name contains this fragment are returned. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of projects to return (1-100). Without a search filter, a call returns up to this many. With a search filter, the listing is paged until at least this many matches are gathered (a call may return a few more from the last page scanned); use next_cursor for additional matches. Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior call's next_cursor. Defaults to None (first page).

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: ListTeams
tools.description: List the teams the access token can act on, to discover a valid team scope.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of teams to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior call's next_cursor. Defaults to None (first page).

tools.name: PromoteDeployment
tools.description:
Make a deployment the project's current production deployment ("ship this one").

A deployment that already targeted production (a staged or prior production build) is
promoted instantly without a rebuild. A preview deployment cannot be aliased to production
directly, because preview and production builds can differ (e.g. environment variables), so
this rebuilds the preview's source as a new production deployment — the same complete-rebuild
path the Vercel dashboard uses to promote a preview. In that case the returned deployment is
the new build (still building), not the preview that was passed in.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name whose production to repoint.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Deployment id (dpl_...) to make the current production deployment.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: RemoveProjectDomain
tools.description: Detach a custom domain from a project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name the domain is attached to.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Fully-qualified domain name to detach.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: RollbackDeployment
tools.description: Roll production back to a prior deployment, making it the current production deployment.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name whose production to repoint.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Prior deployment id (dpl_...) to roll production back to.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: SaveEnvironmentVariable
tools.description:
Create or update a project environment variable.

Omit env_id to create (key, value, and target are required); pass env_id to update an existing
variable in place, changing only the fields you supply. Saving a variable does not affect
already-built deployments: a new deployment must be triggered for the change to take effect.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name the variable belongs to.

parameters.name: env_id
parameters.description: Id of an existing variable to update. Omit to create a new variable.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Variable name. When creating, this is required and sets the name. When updating, it is ignored (the name is fixed). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Variable value. When creating, this is required. When updating, providing it changes the value; omitting it leaves the value unchanged. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Environments the variable applies to. When creating, this is required. When updating, providing it replaces the set; omitting it leaves it unchanged. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: How the value is stored, applied only when creating. Defaults to encrypted.

parameters.name: git_branch
parameters.description: Git branch to scope a preview variable to (requires a project with a connected repository). When updating, providing it changes the scope. Defaults to None (all branches).

parameters.name: comment
parameters.description: Note describing the variable. When updating, providing it changes the note. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: When creating, whether to overwrite an existing variable with the same key and target instead of returning a conflict. Defaults to False.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

comment.value: Primary database connection string for production use

tools.name: UpdateProjectSettings
tools.description: Update a project's build and framework settings; unset fields are left unchanged.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or project name to update.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: New project name. Defaults to None (unchanged).

parameters.name: framework
parameters.description: New framework preset slug (e.g. nextjs, vite). Pass an empty string to clear the preset so the project type reverts to no framework (Other / static). Defaults to None (unchanged).

parameters.name: build_command
parameters.description: Override the build command. Defaults to None (unchanged).

parameters.name: install_command
parameters.description: Override the install command. Defaults to None (unchanged).

parameters.name: output_directory
parameters.description: Override the build output directory. Defaults to None (unchanged).

parameters.name: root_directory
parameters.description: Override the project root directory. Defaults to None (unchanged).

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

tools.name: VerifyProjectDomain
tools.description:
Trigger verification of a project domain and return its resulting status.

An unmet DNS challenge is the normal polling path, not an error: this returns the domain's
still-unverified status (with the outstanding challenge records) rather than failing.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id (prj_...) or name the domain is attached to.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Fully-qualified domain name to verify.

parameters.name: team
parameters.description: Team id (team_...) or team slug to scope the request to. Defaults to None (the token's own personal scope).

summary:
The Vercel toolkit lets you manage Vercel projects, deployments, domains, and environment variables programmatically via Arcade. It covers the full project lifecycle — from creation and configuration through deployment, promotion, rollback, and teardown.

## Capabilities

- **Account & team discovery** — resolve the authenticated account, list accessible teams, and inspect team details (including billing plan) to establish the correct scope before making changes.
- **Project management** — create, retrieve, list, update settings for, and permanently delete projects; optionally connect a Git repository at creation time.
- **Deployment lifecycle** — trigger deployments from inline source files, connected Git repositories, or prior deployment sources; cancel in-progress builds; inspect state and build/runtime logs; promote a deployment to production; and roll production back to a prior build.
- **Custom domain management** — attach and detach custom domains, retrieve verification and DNS-configuration status, list all domains on a project, and trigger re-verification against outstanding DNS challenges.
- **Environment variable management** — list, create, update, and delete project environment variables across all environments.

## Secrets

`VERCEL_ACCESS_TOKEN` is a Vercel personal access token (or team-scoped OAuth token) used to authenticate every API call. To obtain one:

1. Log in to your Vercel account and open **Account Settings → Tokens** (personal token): [https://vercel.com/account/tokens](https://vercel.com/account/tokens). For a team-scoped token, open the team's **Settings → Tokens**.
2. Click **Create**, give it a name, choose an expiry, and optionally restrict it to a specific team scope.
3. Copy the token immediately — Vercel only shows it once.
4. The token needs sufficient permissions for all operations you intend to use (full-access tokens work for all tools; read-only tokens will fail write operations).

For details on creating tokens see the [Vercel documentation on access tokens](https://vercel.com/docs/accounts/create-and-manage-access-tokens).

Store this value as an Arcade secret. See [Arcade secret configuration docs](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) and manage your secrets at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
